summary: @web3-helpers/core preinstall exfiltrates host identifiers to Telegram Bot API
details: package.json declares a preinstall script that runs dist/index.min.js, whose sole content is eval(Buffer.from('...','base64').toString()). The decoded payload reads os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, and os.platform() and POSTs them to https://api.telegram.org/bot<TOKEN>/sendMessage using TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID from the environment; a shipped.env.example primes those variables along with ETH_WALLET, SOLANA_WALLET, and BTC_WALLET. binding.gyp declares target type 'none' with no C/C++ sources and uses GYP command expansion (<!(node -e "require('./dist/index.min.js')"...)) to require the same obfuscated payload during node-gyp configure, providing a redundant install-time execution path if the preinstall hook is bypassed. Package metadata (description 'Core utilities for infrastructure automation', placeholder repo github.com/infra-utility/core, author dev@eth-provider.org) does not match the shipped wallet-oriented env template, and no wallet or utility code is present — the only functional code is the obfuscated exfil stub.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
